compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
Re-home the shared AWS resources previously owned by the now-decommissioned **snap** project into rebar's own terraform and infra documentation. snap and rebar share AWS account `896586841071`. On snap's turndown, 18 shared/rebar-facing resources were left intact but still tracked in snap's dead `snap-demo-tfstate-896586841071` shared state: the GitHub OIDC provider, the `*.solutions.navateam.com` wildcard ACM cert, and the full `auth_host` domain-wide SSO stack (API GW v2 + CloudFront + Lambda + Route53 A/AAAA + cookie-signing SSM secret). rebar functionally depends on the OIDC provider (its `rebar-terraform-plan` drift role trusts it) but does not create it.

## Context
- The OIDC provider (`token.actions.githubusercontent.com`) is a per-account singleton at `arn:aws:iam::896586841071:oidc-provider/token.actions.githubusercontent.com`. rebar's `infra/terraform/iam.tf` assumes it exists; if snap's dead state were ever destroyed it would take the provider with it and break rebar's drift CI. This is the one hard dependency.
- The wildcard cert + auth_host SSO stack are domain-wide and currently idle orphans in snap's dead state. Decision (user): re-home them into rebar so the domain-wide SSO survives.
- Physical resource names are `snap-demo-*`; renaming forces destroy/recreate (new ARNs, CloudFront churn), so names are PRESERVED and only ownership + the `project=snap-demo` tag are moved to rebar.
- Live execution authorized: import into rebar's `rebar-tfstate-896586841071` state, apply the retag, release the resources from snap's shared state.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 18 shared resources are defined in rebar's `infra/terraform/**` and tracked in rebar's own state.
- [ ] `terraform plan` in `infra/terraform` is clean (no destroy/replace of the adopted live resources; no secret-value rotation).
- [ ] Adopted resources carry `Project = rebar`, not `project = snap-demo`.
- [ ] The re-homed resources are removed from snap's `shared/terraform.tfstate` (single-owner).
- [ ] rebar's infra docs describe the OIDC ownership + the SSO stack (adapted runbook).
- [ ] All child stories closed and the code landed on `main` via Gerrit.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Child story "terraform adoption + live import/apply/retag" closed.
- [ ] Child story "infra documentation (OIDC ownership + SSO runbook + ADR)" closed.
- [ ] [operator-attested] Live `terraform apply` completed and `terraform plan` reports no changes for the adopted resources (evidence: apply/plan output recorded as a comment).
- [ ] terraform-drift CI on `main` is green after the change lands.
compiled_state.title: Re-home snap shared AWS infra (OIDC provider, wildcard cert, auth_host SSO) into rebar
